rules:
This card provides Colorless Energy.

When you attach this card from your hand to a Pokémon, draw a card.

Search your deck for up to 2 TAG TEAM cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

The attacks of non-Ultra Beast Pokémon that have any basic Fighting Energy attached to them (both yours and your opponent's) do 10 more damage to the opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). If the attacking player has more Prize cards remaining than their opponent, those attacks do 40 more damage instead.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Play this card as if it were a 30-HP Colorless Basic Pokémon. At any time during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may discard all cards from it and put it on the bottom of your deck.

This card can't retreat. If this card is Knocked Out, your opponent can't take any Prize cards for it.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Shuffle a Pokémon and a Pokémon Tool card from your discard pile into your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

The Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX this card is attached to gets -100 HP, and when it is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, that player takes 1 fewer Prize card.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

You can play this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.

Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX with their Active Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Once during each player's turn, that player may discard a card from their hand. If they do, that player searches their deck for up to 2 Fire Energy cards, reveals them, and puts them into their hand. Then, that player shuffles their deck.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

attacks.name: Brave Buddies
attacks.effect: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

attacks.name: Silver Knight-GX
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Ultra Beast, it is Knocked Out. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Jumping Balloon
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 more damage for each of your opponent's Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX in play.

attacks.name: Puffy Smashers-GX
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep. If this Pokémon has at least 4 extra Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 200 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Chaotic Order-GX
attacks.effect: Turn all of your Prize cards face up. (Those Prize cards remain face up for the rest of the game.) If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Psychic Energy and 1 extra Darkness Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), take 2 Prize cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cross Break-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 170 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It pays no mind to the cold, thanks to its long warm coat. Stoutland in Alola look a little uncomfortable.

attacks.name: Thumping Fall
attacks.effect: Discard any number of Pokémon with a Retreat Cost of exactly 4 from your hand. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Iron Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Tempered underground under high pressure and heat, its body is harder than any metal.

attacks.name: Eleventh Hour Tackle
attacks.effect: If there are 3 or fewer cards in your deck, this attack does 150 more damage.

attacks.name: Drill Bazooka
attacks.effect: Discard the top 4 cards of your deck.

flavorText: It can help in tunnel construction. Its drill has evolved into steel strong enough to bore through iron plates.

attacks.name: Impersonation
attacks.effect: Discard a Supporter card from your hand. If you do, use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack.

attacks.name: Mischievous Hands
attacks.effect: Choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon and put 2 damage counters on each of them.

flavorText: Although it's a quiet, lonely Pokémon, if you try to look at what's under its rag, it will become agitated and resist violently.

attacks.name: Power Cyclone
attacks.effect: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Because it can sense what its foe is thinking, its attacks burst out first, fast, and fierce.

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its thin, balloon-like body is inflated by horribly toxic gases. It reeks when it is nearby.

attacks.name: Mirror Shot
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It's frequently the cause of power outages, which is why some power plants send out electrical signals that it can't stand.

attacks.name: Nuzzle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Volt Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its nature is to store up electricity. Forests where nests of Pikachu live are dangerous, since the trees are so often struck by lightning.
Wishiwashi assemble in this formation to face off against strong foes. It boasts a strength that earned it the name "demon of the sea."

attacks.name: Bubble Hold
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It doesn't like to be taken care of. It's difficult to bond with since it won't listen to its Trainer.

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: One Weavile will trip a Sandshrew and flip it over, and then another Weavile will deal the finishing blow with its sharp claws.

attacks.name: Fire Fling
attacks.effect: Put 4 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Kindle
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: You can tell how it's feeling by the smoke spouting from its shell. Tremendous velocity is a sign of good health.

rules:
You can play this card only if 1 of your Pokémon was Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.

Search your deck for a Pokémon, a Trainer card, and a basic Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards. If you discarded an Energy card in this way, draw 2 more cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for a Pokémon-GX that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn.)

When you play this card, you may discard 2 other cards from your hand. If you do, search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to the Pokémon you evolved in this way.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon of different types and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Discard the top 6 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are basic Energy cards, attach them to 1 of your Benched Dragon Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

When you play this card, you may discard 2 other cards from your hand. If you do, heal 120 damage from the Pokémon you moved to your Bench.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Draw 4 cards.

At the end of this turn, if you have 3 or more cards in your hand, shuffle cards from your hand into your deck until you have 2 cards in your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for a Stadium card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

When you play this card, you may discard 2 other cards from your hand. If you do, you may also search for a Pokémon Tool card and a Special Energy card in this way.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand. You can't choose Cynthia & Caitlin or a card you discarded with the effect of this card.

When you play this card, you may discard another card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Anyone who dares peer into its body to see its spectral ball of fire will have their spirit stolen away.

attacks.name: Ominous Eyes
attacks.effect: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: It doggedly pursues its prey wherever it goes. However, the chase is abandoned at sunrise.

attacks.name: Power Cyclone
attacks.effect: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Because it can sense what its foe is thinking, its attacks burst out first, fast, and fierce.

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The cheerful spirit of its Trainer gives it energy for its psychokinetic power. It spins and dances when happy.

attacks.name: Teleport

attacks.name: Creepy Wind

flavorText: It's said that while this Pokémon has the power to predict the future, it's not powerful enough to change the future it sees.

attacks.name: Future Sight
attacks.effect: Look at the top 4 cards of either player's deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: Although it still can't fly, its jumping power is outstanding. It jumps way up into trees and plucks the buds from the branches.

attacks.name: Balloon Burst
attacks.effect: Discard this Pokémon and all cards attached to it.

flavorText: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its thin, balloon-like body is inflated by horribly toxic gases. It reeks when it is nearby.

attacks.name: Cosmic Burn

attacks.name: Light of the Protector-GX
attacks.effect: If you played Lillie's Full Force from your hand during this turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to each of your Pokémon during your opponent's next turn. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When it's surprised or agitated, the 14 fur spikes on its back will stand up involuntarily.

flavorText: When it's surprised or agitated, the 14 fur spikes on its back will stand up involuntarily.

flavorText: When the bacteria living inside its antennae absorb Lanturn's bodily fluids, a strong luminescent effect is produced.

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand.

flavorText: Its two antennae glow softly to lure in prey, making it a useful Pokémon for night fishing.
Its lungs contain an organ that creates electricity. The crackling sound of electricity can be heard when it exhales.
Delicate equipment can malfunction in areas inhabited by Magneton, which send out mysterious electrical signals.

attacks.name: Mirror Shot
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It's frequently the cause of power outages, which is why some power plants send out electrical signals that it can't stand.

attacks.name: Nuzzle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Powerful Spark
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Lightning Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: As electricity builds up inside its body, it becomes more aggressive. One theory is that the electricity buildup is actually causing stress.

attacks.name: Nuzzle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Volt Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its nature is to store up electricity. Forests where nests of Pikachu live are dangerous, since the trees are so often struck by lightning.

attacks.name: Liquidation
attacks.effect: During your next turn, the Defending Pokémon takes 60 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It usually passes its time in the water. When its belly is full, it stores its subdued prey in the water bubble on its head.
When two Dewpider meet, they display their water bubbles to each other. Then the one with the smaller bubble gets out of the other's way.

attacks.name: School Storm
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Wishiwashi and Wishiwashi-GX in play.

attacks.name: Massive Catch-GX
attacks.effect: Look at the top 12 cards of your deck and put any number of Basic Pokémon you find there onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Wishiwashi assemble in this formation to face off against strong foes. It boasts a strength that earned it the name "demon of the sea."

attacks.name: Frozen Wings
attacks.effect: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dazzling Blizzard
attacks.effect: If you have any Stadium card in play, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: This legendary ice Pokémon waits for a hero to fill in the missing parts of its body with truth or ideals.

attacks.name: Tailwind
attacks.effect: Attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Despite their elegant appearance, they can flap their wings strongly and fly for thousands of miles.

attacks.name: Aerial Ace
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: When attacked, it uses its feathers to splash water, escaping under cover of the spray.

attacks.name: Flail Around
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans and warns others of danger.
When the water warms, they inflate the flotation sac on their heads and drift languidly on the sea in packs.

attacks.name: Recall
attacks.effect: Choose an attack from 1 of this Pokémon's previous Evolutions and use it as this attack.

attacks.name: Aquafall
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It swims as fast as a jet boat. The edges of its wings are sharp and can slice apart drifting ice.

attacks.name: Direct Dive

flavorText: It lives alone, away from others. Apparently, every one of them believes it is the most important.

attacks.name: Bubble Hold
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It doesn't like to be taken care of. It's difficult to bond with since it won't listen to its Trainer.

attacks.name: High Water
attacks.effect: Attach 2 Water Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Swirling Waves
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: A mythical Pokémon said to have swelled the seas with rain and tidal waves. It battled with Groudon.

attacks.name: Cold Snap
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't play any Trainer cards from their hand during their next turn. If 1 of your Pokémon used Cold Snap during your last turn, this attack can't be used.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It shatters ice with its big tusks. Its thick blubber repels not only the cold, but also enemy attacks.
It has a very sensitive nose. It touches new things with its nose to examine them.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: They can't swim well yet, and they move much faster by rolling. When they're happy, they clap fins.
They can't swim well yet, and they move much faster by rolling. When they're happy, they clap fins.

attacks.name: Frosty Typhoon
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Frosty Typhoon during your next turn.

flavorText: It freezes its prey and chews them whole. However, it prefers to eat Pokémon like Vanillite that are already frozen.

attacks.name: Continuous Headbutt

flavorText: It feeds mainly on ice and snow. It's only able to survive in a limited number of places in the warm Alola region.
They eat so many fish Pokémon that when Wailord become too numerous, fishermen have to chase them off.
It swims along with its mouth open and swallows down seawater along with its food. It sprays excess water out of its nostrils.

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: One Weavile will trip a Sandshrew and flip it over, and then another Weavile will deal the finishing blow with its sharp claws.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: They will cooperate to steal eggs from the nests of bird Pokémon, but fights break out to determine which one gets to eat the eggs.

attacks.name: Refreshing Rain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Clean, clear waters are its usual habitat. When it's about to be attacked by an invading enemy, it dives into the water to hide.

attacks.name: Energy Loop
attacks.effect: Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

attacks.name: Confusion Wave

flavorText: Using psychokinesis gives it a headache, so it normally passes the time spacing out and doing as little as possible.
If you carelessly approach it because it's cute, the boss of the pack, Ninetales, will appear and freeze you.

attacks.name: Splash Maker
attacks.effect: You may attach up to 3 Water Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like. If you do, heal 50 damage from those Pokémon for each card you attached to them in this way.

flavorText: The males are usually lazy, but when attacked by a strong foe, a male will protect its friends with no regard for its own safety.
When they're young, they live with a pride. Once they're able to hunt prey on their own, they're kicked out and have to make their own way.

attacks.name: Backfire
attacks.effect: Put 2 Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

attacks.name: Massive Heat Wave-GX
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from each of your opponent's Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: When battling opponents, it sprays fire from its five horns. The max temperature of the flames can reach nearly 5,500 degrees Fahrenheit.
It can throw a fire punch by setting its fists on fire with its fiery chin. It cares deeply about its friends.
The more it eats, the more fuel it has to make the fire in its stomach stronger. This fills it with even more power.
It can deftly dodge its foe's attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.
This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

attacks.name: Fire Fling

attacks.name: Kindle
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rally Back

flavorText: A Pokémon that races across the land. It is said that one is born every time a new volcano appears.

attacks.name: Stomp Off
attacks.effect: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its brittle shell occasionally spouts intense flames that circulate throughout its body.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If it inhales deeply, that's a sign it's about to attack. Prepare to be hit by flames of over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit!

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.effect: You may search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Crimson Flame Pillar-GX
attacks.effect: Attach 5 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like. attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It nocks its arrow quills and shoots them at opponents. When it simply can't afford to miss, it tugs the vine on its head to improve its focus.

attacks.name: Wing Flick
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This narcissistic Pokémon is a clean freak. If you don't groom it diligently, it will stop listening to you.

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable.

attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Their coloring changes according to the season and can be slightly affected by the temperature and humidity as well.

attacks.name: Improvisational Performance
attacks.effect: If you have exactly 1 card in your hand, this attack does 100 more damage. If you have exactly 3 cards in your hand, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. If you have exactly 6 cards in your hand, this attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Collect

attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The bunches of fruit growing around the necks of Tropius in Alola are especially sweet compared to those in other regions.

attacks.name: Poison Tentacles
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Normally, it lived on shallow sea shoals. When the tide went out, this Pokémon came up on land to search for prey.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: In ancient times, it lived in warm seas. It disguised itself as seaweed to ambush its prey and devoured them whole when they got close.

flavorText: It's proud of its thick horn. In Alola, its biggest rival is Vikavolt, which it's always fighting with.

attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Sweet Scent
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Solar Plant-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.
